content: For TSPTW (TSP with Time Windows), the practical approach for travel apps is: (1) Nearest Neighbor Heuristic as construction phase, then (2) 2-opt local search for improvement. This gives near-optimal results in polynomial time, suitable for 10-30 POIs. Academic alternatives like Ant Colony System and Brainstorm Optimization exist but are complex to implement. The Team Orienteering Problem with Time Windows (TOPTW) is the academic frame for multi-day itinerary recommendation. Key constraint: each POI has earliest open time and latest close time. Algorithm must skip POIs that cannot be visited given travel time from previous location.
